event: Portrait of Charles De Saint Albin, Archbishop of Cambrai
symbols: pectoral cross — the archbishop's authority and devotion to Christ crucified
elaborate vestments — the dignity of ecclesiastical office
book held in hand — the bishop's duty to teach and guard the faith
classical columns — the institutional strength of the Church
description: A formal portrait of a man seated in a grand interior with classical columns and rich gold drapery. He wears elaborate clerical vestments: a white surplice or rochet over a red stole, with lace at the cuffs. A gold pectoral cross hangs from his neck. He has a long curled wig in the French style. His left hand rests on a large blue-bound book. His expression is composed and authoritative. The painting is richly colored in whites, reds, golds, and blues.

_meta.title: Charles De Saint Albin, Archbishop of Cambrai
